title: Emmy Race Reset Following Critics Choice Awards
text: The 2026 Critics Choice Awards produced significant momentum shifts in several Emmy categories. Rhea Seehorn claimed Best Drama Actress for "Pluribus," ending her long awards bridesmaid status. The Apple TV series, created by Vince Gilligan, has become the platform's most-watched original. Seehorn portrays one of Earth's few unaffected humans in a story about an alien virus. Backstage, she noted: "I'm acutely aware that there are many people who did beautiful work this season that were not nominated." With back-to-back Golden Globe and Critics Choice Awards victories, Seehorn has solidified her position as the lead contender heading into September's Emmy Awards. The competing actresses in the category include Britt Lower for "Severance," Keri Russell for "The Diplomat," Kathy Bates for "Matlock," Helen Mirren for "Mobland," and Bella Ramsey for "The Last of Us."
